title: Thiosalicylamide as a Reagent in Inorganic Analysis
author: S.C. SHOME
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Presidency College, Calcutta 700012 

 Thiosalicylamide has been introduced as a suitable reagent in the (a) gravimetry of the platinum metals, gold, mercury, copper, selenium and tellurium, (b) spectrophotometry of the platinum metals, gold, mercury, thallium, selenium, oxidising agents e.g., eerie sulphate, hydrogen peroxide, potassium permanganate and potassium dichro­mate and (c) indirect titrimetry of palladium, platinum, rhodium, copper and the oxidising agents. Thiosalicylarnide is further applied as a precipitant for bismuth, cadmium, mercury and lead as sulphides from homogeneous solu­tions.
